channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PMD Forecasts Intense Heatwave Across Pakistan Between May 7 and 11
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a warning for severe heatwave conditions across central and southern regions of Pakistan from May 7 to 11, 2026. The extreme temperatures are be...

item.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 to Feature Expert Panel on Early-Stage M&A Strategies
item.description: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, scheduled for October 13–15 at San Francisco’s Moscone West, will feature a dedicated panel aimed at founders navigating mergers and acquisitions (M&A). The conference is offe...

item.title: US and Iran Near Agreement to Restore Strait of Hormuz and Ease Tensions
item.description: Recent developments suggest that the United States and Iran are approaching a potential agreement to end ongoing hostilities. Iran is currently reviewing a concise memorandum of understanding that cou...

item.title: Tim Hortons Reports Strong Q1 Sales Despite Challenging Conditions
item.description: Restaurant Brands International, which owns Tim Hortons, reported that its first-quarter sales remained robust despite challenging external factors. The company noted that colder-than-usual weather an...

item.title: 19-year-old ex-Army cadet jailed for stalking two young women and threatening violence
item.description: Dihan Rahman, a 19-year-old former Army cadet from West London, has been sentenced to four-and-a-half years in prison, with an additional three-year extended licence, following a sustained campaign of...

item.title: Five professional dancers depart Strictly Come Dancing ahead of 2026 series
item.description: The BBC has announced that five professional dancers will be leaving Strictly Come Dancing as preparations for the 2026 series begin. Karen Hauer, the show's longest-serving female professional, depar...

item.title: Narito ang mga official na winning number ng PCSO lotto para sa Mayo 6, 2026
item.description: Naglabas ngayon ng mga resulta ang Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) para sa mga lotto draw noong Mayo 6, 2026, kabilang ang mga sikat na laro tulad ng Grand Lotto 6/55, Mega Lotto 6/45, Su...

item.title: CRDB Foundation launches UDSM initiative to turn student research into businesses
item.description: CRDB Bank Foundation imezindua IMBEJU–UDSM Startup Challenge 2026 kwenye Chuo Kikuu cha Dar es Salaam (UDSM) ili kusaidia wanafunzi na wahitimu wa hivi karibuni kugeuza miradi ya kitaaluma, utafiti na...

item.title: Dar City Makes History as First Tanzanian Club in BAL Playoffs
item.description: Dar es Salaam's own Dar City Basketball Club is making history as they prepare to face Angola's Petro de Luanda in the quarter-finals of the Basketball Africa League (BAL) playoffs. The knockout stage...

item.title: Relief efforts reach families displaced by severe storm in Nelson Mandela Bay
item.description: A severe level 8 storm hit Nelson Mandela Bay, causing significant flooding and displacing many families in townships such as Kwazakhele and Dongweni. Relief efforts quickly mobilised, with the charit...

item.title: Analysis of Rising Separatist Sentiment in Alberta Amid Federal Policies
item.description: The article by Carson Jerema in the National Post examines the growing separatist sentiment in Alberta, linking it to perceived neglect and punitive policies by the federal Liberal government. Jerema ...

item.title: Supreme Court Prioritises Hearing on Constitutional Validity of Election Commission Appointments Law
item.description: The Supreme Court of India on Wednesday refused to adjourn hearings concerning petitions challenging the 2023 law on the appointment of Election Commissioners, emphasising that the issue is 'more impo...

item.title: KUCCPS Urges Students to Submit Applications Before Deadline to Secure University and TVET Places
item.description: Students across Kenya are scrambling to complete their university and college applications as the KUCCPS portal closes at midnight today. Agnes Mercy Wahome, CEO of KUCCPS, has issued a final reminder...

item.title: Iran Affirms Participation in 2026 World Cup Despite US Visa Restrictions
item.description: The president of the Iranian Football Federation, Mehdi Taj, don yan say FIFA be the correct authority for the 2026 World Cup, no be US government. This talk come as wahala dey about travel restrictio...

item.title: FCT Minister insists on reclaiming Jabi Lake land for proper development
item.description: Nyesom Wike, Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), don yan say the government no go gree any shanty or substandard development around Jabi Lake for Abuja. Dis talk come after Rev Serah Omak...

item.title: Pakistan launches Capital Market Development Fund to boost financial literacy and investor participation
item.description: On May 6, 2026, Pakistan’s key capital market institutions jointly established the Capital Market Development Fund (CMDF) to promote financial literacy, broaden financial inclusion, and encourage reta...

item.title: Katie Simpson review exposes systemic failures and misogyny within PSNI
item.description: A recent review into the handling of Katie Simpson's murder has revealed deep systemic issues within the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI), describing failures as both 'institutional misogyny'...

item.title: Kenya’s Sarah Mose Wins Bronze and Sets National Record at Africa Swimming Championships
item.description: On Wednesday morning, Kenya’s rising swimming star Sarah Mose made waves at the Africa Aquatics Swimming Championships held in Oran, Algeria. The 19-year-old claimed bronze in the women's 100 metres f...

item.title: US and Iran Near Agreement Amid Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Negotiations between the United States and Iran appear to be advancing toward a potential one-page memorandum intended to end the ongoing conflict. While officials from the Trump administration remain...

item.title: Australian authorities prepare for the return of ISIS-affiliated citizens and their children
item.description: Four women, often referred to as 'ISIS brides', along with nine children are expected to arrive in Australia from Syria shortly. The Australian Federal Police (AFP) are on standby to arrest certain in...
